embedded.description: EmbeddedSurgewave throughput (1000000 messages, 100 bytes, batch 1000, Memory storage)

surgewaveNativeProtocol.description: Surgewave Native Protocol (binary, optimized for Surgewave broker)

kafkaProtocol.description: Kafka Wire Protocol (compatible with Apache Kafka clients)

transportComparison.description: TCP vs QUIC transport comparison (50000 messages, 100 bytes, direct loopback)

direct.description: QUIC direct (no proxy, 0% loss), ~5% slower than TCP

loss1pct.description: QUIC through LossyUdpProxy (1% packet loss, 0ms latency)

peerTransportOverhead.notes: Factory lookup is ~9ns (ConcurrentDictionary). ConnectAsync dominated by TCP handshake, not abstraction overhead.

tcpBaselineVerification.description: Post-IPeerTransport migration baseline verification (1000000 messages, 100 bytes, batch 1000, Memory storage)
tcpBaselineVerification.verdict: No regression — both metrics within 10% tolerance

replication.description: 2-broker embedded cluster with RF=2, TCP inter-broker (100000 messages, 100 bytes, batch 1000, Memory storage)

protocolComparison.description: Surgewave Native vs Kafka Wire Protocol on same embedded broker (1000000 messages, 100 bytes)

environment.cpu: Intel Core i7-1360P (13th Gen, 12 cores / 16 threads)

changes: MemoryLogSegment: Dictionary + List instead of SortedList for O(1) insertion
MemoryLogSegment: SpinLock instead of Monitor for reduced overhead
LogManager: Pooled completion sources using IValueTaskSource
RecordBatchSerializer: Added SerializeMessagesPooled API for zero-copy path
